codeReview.summary: Solid implementation of the Walk-Forward tab (541 lines in walkforward-tab.js, 283 lines of CSS in compare.html, 129-line fixture JSON). All 11 VAL-WFUI assertions are covered: Plotly line chart with train (blue solid) and test (yellow dashed) lines, shaded delta bands, pass/fail window coloring (green/red shapes), verdict badge (STABLE/CONDITIONALLY_STABLE/UNSTABLE with correct color mapping), 7 summary stats, click/hover detail panel with all required fields, pass threshold indicator line, worst window annotation with arrow+circle highlight, degradation flag warning, empty windows state, and missing data error state. Dark theme applied correctly via shared plotlyLayout() helper. Code follows all project conventions: CSS custom properties, monospace font for values, no var, template literals, single global app state, Plotly.purge() lifecycle, proper script tag ordering.

issues.description: Pass threshold line is computed as meanTrain * (1 - threshold/100) using the mean of ALL train values. The actual per-window pass/fail determination uses per-window delta_pct vs the threshold, not a global mean. The threshold line is an approximation that could slightly misrepresent the actual pass boundary for individual windows. This is a reasonable visualization tradeoff but worth noting.
Handoff reports '380 lines' but the actual file is 541 lines. Minor documentation inaccuracy in the handoff, not a code issue.

Template literal HTML injection in showWindowDetail() and renderWFEmpty() inserts data values (regime_tags, period strings) directly into innerHTML without escaping. Low risk since data comes from pre-generated JSON fixtures, but could be an XSS vector if data source changes.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The handoff reported 380 lines for walkforward-tab.js but the actual file is 541 lines. AGENTS.md doesn't have guidance on accurate file-size reporting in handoffs. Minor issue but could affect future reviews' ability to cross-reference handoff claims.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: Handoff salientSummary says '380 lines', git show dd49ca2 --stat shows 541 insertions for walkforward-tab.js

summary: Walk-forward-visualization passes review. All 11 VAL-WFUI assertions are implemented: two-line chart with visual distinction, delta bands, pass/fail coloring, verdict badge, 7 summary stats, detail panel on hover/click, threshold indicator, worst window highlight, degradation warning, empty state handling, and missing file error state. Dark theme correctly applied via shared Plotly layout helper. CSS uses project design tokens. No blocking issues found. Three non-blocking observations: threshold line is an approximation from global mean rather than per-window, handoff line count is inaccurate (380 vs 541), and template literal HTML injection is unescaped (low risk with pre-generated data).
